purpose: Phase 8 better-tuned Smart AI v2 to address Phase 7 sim artifacts (WS-H-3, WS-H-4). Tests whether action triangle balance finding holds when both sides manage stamina properly and use PP-294-aware Feint alternation. Key finding: Phase 7's 'action triangle inverts dominance' was partly stamina-management artifact; pool advantage is structurally dominant when both sides play tactically.

entries.section: §Expected Value table, TN 7 row (post-F13 fix commit 721acc15)

entries.quoted_text: Player chooses N dice (minimum 3) to Offence for feint; remainder to Defence

entries.section: Phase 8 sim refinement; canonical is just action cost = 3, sim heuristic adjusted from Phase 7 (was 5) to fix high-stam-skip artifact
entries.quoted_text: Phase 8 raised the Smart AI's Take Breath trigger threshold from <=5 to <=8 stamina to address Phase 7's discrete-action-skip artifact where high-stamina builds bypassed the 3-5 trigger window and exhausted at round 5. Canonical rule unchanged (action cost = 3); only the sim AI heuristic adjusted.

entries.section: §Damage Formula — Light Blade Cut vs None

entries.quoted_text: Critical Hit (net hits >= 4): weapon modifier doubled before applying armour reduction. (PP-717 — raised from >= 3.)

entries.section: Architecture C sim baseline (matches v27 + Phases 4-7)

entries.value: Roll Off vs Def; on margin>0, opponent loses [margin] dice next round; floor 1D; non-stacking
entries.quoted_text: Non-stacking: Successive Feints reset reduction to current margin; do not accumulate

entries.value: Alternate Feint (even rounds) and Strike (odd rounds) when pool-disadvantaged
entries.section: PP-294 non-stacking implies alternation is optimal
entries.quoted_text: PP-294 says reductions don't stack across consecutive Feints. Therefore Feint-spam wastes the lever. Optimal use: Feint to setup pool reduction, then Strike to exploit the reduced opponent pool. Phase 8 Smart v2 implements this alternation.

sim_limitations: Smart v2 alternation is heuristic (rd % 2); not action-history-aware. Phase 9 could implement pending-reduction tracking for principled exploit timing.
Initiative-knowledge asymmetry (PP-232 — init holder declares last) not modeled
Single weapon (Light Blade Cut) and armour (None)
No Distance, Disarm, Establish Distance, Fibonacci group bonus modeled
Symmetric AI assumption may not match real play variance
key_findings: Phase 7's '34.9% Feint inverts dominance' was partly stamina-management artifact (Underdog Fast was strike_only and exhausted at round 5)
Smart v2 symmetric play: Agi-dominance at 100% Fast (pool advantage structurally dominant at 17D vs 11D)
Smart v2 symmetric play: End-dominance at 63.7% Tough (real, moderate)
Symmetric Agi 3 vs Agi 3 produces 51.2% (sim calibrated; deviations are real signal)
Combat Balance Note's 34.9% claim needs revision (misleading without stamina-asymmetry caveat)
Decision A's revert of PP-717 D2 may have insufficient compensation from action triangle alone
